what_it_does: It acts as a shared research-wave anchor document that frames a GPU/serving/loadout problem, proposes a measure-then-compute resource model, lists ideas and hard parts, points to existing machinery to extend, and instructs readers to stress-test every claim against what vLLM 0.21 actually does.
observations: Concrete failure anchor: co-resident brain hit Available KV cache memory: -3.78 GiB at gpu_util 0.5, while solo it was +1.87 GiB.
Proposes replacing hand-typed memory knobs with computed budgets derived from a model's measured profile plus live available headroom.
Explicit epistemic markers are required: Observed / Inferred / External-prior-art / My-idea, with a directive to contradict naive claims.
Wants to extend existing machinery: auto_gpu_util in ops/cli/gpu.py, serveconfig.py, runtime/capabilities, and services.json loadout variants.

Lists honest hard parts: hybrid pool padding over-prediction, prefix-cache hit-rate collapse below 1056 tokens, sleep-mode breakage, per-seq state uncertainty, cuda-graph blind spot, non-vLLM co-tenants, WSL2 measurement quirks.
Includes measured FP8 numbers and a list of verified-present vLLM 0.21 flags.
Distinguishes conversational vs worker shapes as two declared loadouts of the same model.
Closes with a spirit note asking agents to bring what is actually there, not what the anchor hopes.
standouts: The -3.78 GiB vs +1.87 GiB co-resident/solo KV cache measurement is the central pain signal.
The explicit instruction to ground or refute every claim in real vLLM 0.21 behavior, not in the anchor's hopes.
The decision to build on existing auto_gpu_util/serveconfig/runtime/capabilities/services.json rather than create a parallel system.
conventions: Research-wave anchor format with numbered sections and a required read-in-full-then-SCOPE.md ordering.
Heavy inline cross-references to companion files and existing code paths using file:line notation.
Mixes narrative framing, measured data, open questions, and agent-facing spirit notes in one document.
Uses an imperative, epistemically humble tone toward agents.
concerns: Many claims are explicitly marked as ideas-to-be-challenged, so the file is intentionally speculative.
Dense jargon and many moving parts may make it hard to act on without the companion SCOPE.md.
The 'expansion ratio > 1' spirit instruction is evocative but not operationally defined.
Some measured numbers come from a specific WSL2/Ada setup, limiting how far they can be generalized without re-measurement.
notes: A research-wave manifesto that anchors a vLLM GPU loadout problem in a real negative-KV-cache OOM and asks agents to verify or refute a computed-budget, loadout-driven replacement for hand-tuned memory knobs.
questions: What exactly does the companion SCOPE.md contain, and how does it differ from this anchor?
Which of the referenced code changes are already committed versus still planned?
How will the profiler parse vLLM boot output robustly across model formats and knob sweeps?
What will the final interface for MCP agents and the RHM to select a loadout look like?
Has sleep-mode been verified to work on the FP8 model in this WSL2 environment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a shared anchor for a research wave and to frame the problem, insight, and shape; the file does exactly that, while also embedding concrete numbers, epistemic markers, and open questions, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: Measure before compute; no hand-typed resource parameters.
Epistemic humility and active stress-testing of the anchor itself.
Extend existing machinery rather than rebuild.
Prefer vLLM/industry built-ins over custom bandaids.
Live headroom plus measured profile equals computed serve flags.
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a rigorous, evidence-based redesign of GPU model loadout/configuration so that CLI, MCP agents, and the RHM can pick a declarative loadout and the system computes the exact vLLM flags needed to fit co-resident models without manual tuning.
novelty: The combination of a measured _profile schema, an absolute KV byte budget, enforce_eager, and declarative loadouts selected by multiple actors, all explicitly tied to extending an existing auto_gpu_util/serveconfig stack rather than building a new orchestrator.
contribution: It contributes a concrete problem anchor (negative KV cache memory under co-residency), a proposed vocabulary of measured profile fields and loadout shapes, an explicit list of hard parts and verified vLLM flags to investigate, and a directive to ground claims in real vLLM behavior — all of which a fused system would keep as the framing for the research wave.
summary_for_embedding: ANCHOR.md is a research-wave anchor that frames a vLLM GPU serving/loadout problem around a real negative-KV-cache OOM, proposes a measure-then-compute resource model with declarative loadouts, and instructs agents to verify every claim against vLLM 0.21 behavior.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to align a team of agents on a rigorous, evidence-based redesign of GPU model loadouts by replacing hand-typed memory knobs with computed budgets derived from measured model profiles and live headroom.
